summary: Session 2 of 4: wired the Getting Started form's three actions end to end, reusing the existing engines. (1) Build project structure: structure-only no-prompt scaffold (spec D5) - new renderStructureBootstrap + structureOnlyContext in consumerBootstrap.ts render ONLY the three engine files + docs/dabbler/start-here.md (NO starter session set: there is no title prompt, and materializing a set would flip the dual-mode Explorer to list mid-form); ScaffoldDeps.structureOnly switches the shared Set 058 writer; buildProjectStructureNoPrompt scaffolds the OPEN workspace folder (folder-picker fallback when none), silent git init, tier from the form radio, tier-aware venv+router install with the Lightweight router-config divergence preserved. (2) Import plan: planImport.ts split into importPlanFromFile + copyPlanningPrompt with an injectable PlanImportUi; the dabbler.importPlan QuickPick delegates to the same functions. (3) Build session sets: buildSessionGenPrompt gains a parallel option (D4/D7 - worktree + prerequisites: decomposition guidance in the copied prompt text); copySessionSetGenPrompt shared by the command and the form. Form-host protocol: GettingStartedActionMsg (5 action ids + tier/parallel riders, separate channel from the executeCommand allowlist) routed through pure routeGettingStartedAction (unknown actions ignored, tier narrowed via asTier defaulting full, parallel === true) with makeGettingStartedHandlers binding the real implementations; CustomSessionSetsView refreshes its snapshot after every handled action. client.js wires the data-gs-action buttons with persistent gsState (tier/parallel survive re-renders) and a disabled-until-repaint double-click guard; no-folder CTA wired to showOpenDialog -> vscode.openFolder. 16 new unit tests (gettingStartedActions.test.ts); full suite 676 passing / 2 pre-existing stub failures; esbuild + tsc clean. No release this session (0.29.0 bump is Session 4).
verification_notes: Cross-provider verification routed to gpt-5-4 (OpenAI), 2 rounds, $0.1348 routed verification spend. R1 ISSUES_FOUND with one Major (D7 in-form worktree info note not rendered when the parallel checkbox is checked) - DISPUTED as out-of-session-scope: the round-1 prompt's spec excerpt ended at the Session 2 plan, so the verifier could not see that the spec assigns the D7 note to Session 3 step 2 verbatim ('Parallel worktree info (D7): checking the box surfaces the worktree note'); Session 2's plan scopes the checkbox to the copied prompt text only. R2, fed the Session 3 spec text, accepted the disposition and returned VERIFIED with no remaining Session-2-scope issues. Structured findings persisted to s2-issues.json (schemaVersion 2, resolution_status advisory-disagreement, resolved_in_round 2). Orchestrator-made design decision flagged to and endorsed by the verifier in BOTH rounds: the structure-only scaffold (no starter session set) is the correct reading of D1/D4/D5. Next-orchestrator recommendation routed to gemini-pro (analysis, $0.0023).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (gemini-pro): the claude-code engine successfully orchestrated the two preceding sessions with similar TypeScript and VS Code extension tasks; continuing maintains context and leverages a proven workflow for this codebase. Session 3 adds the D6 env-var validation, the D7 worktree note (carrying s2-issues.json's deferred finding), the D8 static instructions doc, and the old-path retirement.
